how_it_works.summary: Tell AI what to do. Rondo dispatches it. Get the result back.

three_steps.name: DEFINE
three_steps.what: Write a Task with instruction (Do), context_files (Read), done_when (Done)
three_steps.example: Task(name="scan", instruction="Search for new trials", done_when="Results returned as JSON")

three_steps.name: DISPATCH
three_steps.what: Rondo sends it to Claude via subprocess (or MCP via rondo_run)

three_steps.name: RESULT
three_steps.what: Get back TaskResult with status, raw_output, cost, duration

polling_tiers.description: Three tiers for checking background dispatch status. Use the cheapest that gives you what you need.

tiers.name: heartbeat
tiers.use_when: Tight polling loop — just checking 'still running?'

tiers.name: brief
tiers.use_when: Normal polling — want readable status + counts

tiers.name: full
tiers.response: Full JSON with task results, raw_output, cost, duration
tiers.use_when: Task is done — get the actual results

quick_examples.name: Search for data
quick_examples.task: Task(name="find-trials", instruction="Search BioMCP for Usher Syndrome trials. Return as JSON array.", done_when="All results returned as JSON.")

quick_examples.name: Review code
quick_examples.task: Task(name="review", instruction="Review src/main.py for bugs and security issues.", context_files=["src/main.py"], done_when="Findings listed.")

quick_examples.name: Generate a report
quick_examples.task: Task(name="report", instruction="Read the test results and write a summary report.", context_files=["reports/test-results.json"], done_when="Report written to reports/summary.md")

quick_examples.name: Scan and update DB
quick_examples.task: Task(name="db-update", instruction="Query the API for new records. Add any new ones to the SQLite database.", done_when="New records added. Count reported.")

quick_examples.name: Background dispatch with cheap polling
quick_examples.note: brief=True returns {status, done_count, error_count} only. Use brief=False when done to get full results.

quick_examples.name: One-off inline task (no round file)
quick_examples.task: rondo_run(prompt="Search for new trials", dry_run=False, model="haiku")
quick_examples.run: Returns result directly — no polling needed for sync dispatch.

quick_examples.name: Dispatch to Gemini instead of Claude
quick_examples.task: Task(name="gemini-review", instruction="Review this code.", context_files=["src/main.py"], done_when="Findings listed.")

quick_examples.name: Multi-provider parallel review (get consensus)
quick_examples.task: rondo_multi_review(prompt='Review this PR for security issues.', providers='["gemini", "grok", "mistral"]', tier='high', dry_run=False)  # bare names resolve via tier; or pass explicit like 'gemini:gemini-pro-latest'
quick_examples.run: Returns list of per-provider results. Each has provider, status, raw_output.
quick_examples.note: Use for cross-validation — catches issues one model might miss.

quick_examples.name: Check provider health before dispatching
quick_examples.run: Shows all configured providers: UP/DOWN status + latency. Healthy fallback auto-selected if primary is DOWN.

quick_examples.name: Run a model-comparison experiment matrix (REQ-113)
quick_examples.run: Shows the full grid (model x effort x context x replicate) + cost estimate WITHOUT spending. Drop --dry-run to execute under the hard budget_usd ceiling. Then: rondo matrix report NAME (blind-coded if blind:true) and rondo matrix reveal NAME.

quick_examples.name: Catch retired models before they 404
quick_examples.run: Fetches every provider's live model list, reports OK/STALE per configured tier + NEW models. First run found the entire grok-3 family retired (now grok-4.3). Never auto-edits config.

quick_examples.name: See learned model performance (auto-tune)
quick_examples.run: 7-day learned scores per model (success/cost/latency). Feeds recommend_model's learned tier: manual override -> curated default -> learned best -> sonnet.

quick_examples.task: Watchdog sweep: drift + retry queue + 7d reliability, machine-readable
quick_examples.name: Nightly watchdog sweep

quick_examples.task: Install diagnosis: is this setup healthy + exactly what to fix (free)
quick_examples.name: Install diagnosis
Golden five (first hour)
quick_examples.task: Guided five-command first hour, each verified live

mcp_tools.name: rondo_chain
mcp_tools.description: Pipeline: step N output → step N+1 input

mcp_tools.name: rondo_cloud
mcp_tools.description: Dispatch to cloud providers (Gemini/Grok/Mistral/Anthropic/OpenAI optional) with profile + tier + cost cap

mcp_tools.name: rondo_multi_review
mcp_tools.description: Send same prompt to N providers in parallel — returns per-provider results + merged findings. Accepts bare provider names (["gemini", "grok"]) resolved via tier, or explicit ["gemini:gemini-pro-latest"]. Default tier=high.

mcp_tools.name: rondo_review_file
mcp_tools.description: Review a file with 2+ cloud providers. Pass file path, optional tier (high/default/low) and providers.

mcp_tools.name: rondo_review_codebase
mcp_tools.description: Deep codebase review: reads multiple source files, batches them with architecture context, sends actual code to AI providers. Calibrated per-provider prompts. RONDO-215.

mcp_tools.name: rondo_doctor
mcp_tools.description: Install diagnosis: config, keys (redacted last-4), registry drift, dirs, versions. Zero cost; healthy flag + fix hints.

mcp_tools.name: rondo_fleet
mcp_tools.description: Watchdog sweep: drift + retry-queue + 7d reliability vs 95% target. refresh=true re-pulls catalogs. Never notifies.

mcp_tools.name: rondo_verify
mcp_tools.description: REQ-115 verified execution: rondo checks a dispatch's declared postconditions (files/cmd) itself

mcp_tools.name: rondo_jury
mcp_tools.description: REQ-118 cross-vendor jury: DIFFERENT vendors independently judge an artifact; accepted only if every reached juror agrees; disagreement is surfaced as the signal

providers.name: claude
providers.routing: Default for all Claude model names (no prefix needed)

providers.name: grok
providers.description: xAI Grok via Chat Completions-compatible API

providers.name: mistral
providers.description: Mistral AI via Chat Completions-compatible API

providers.name: anthropic
providers.description: Anthropic API directly (not Claude Code subprocess) — for API key billing, cost caps
providers.routing: Use 'anthropic:' prefix (distinct from default 'claude' subprocess path)

providers.name: ollama
providers.description: Local LLM via Ollama (no API key, zero cost)
providers.routing: Use 'local:' prefix OR auto-detected from model name (llama, qwen, etc.)

dispatch.description: Send tasks to Claude Code via claude -p

history.description: Dispatch telemetry — cost, duration, model, status

notifications.description: Notify on round completion or dispatch failure

live_mode.description: Present tasks one at a time for human review

cloud_dispatch.description: Dispatch to cloud AI providers with health check + automatic fallback (REQ-109)
cloud_dispatch.tiers: provider:high → best_model, provider:default → default_model, provider:low → cheap_model (from config)
cloud_dispatch.fallback: If primary provider is down, auto-routes to fallback= from config.toml
cloud_dispatch.multi_provider: rondo_cloud() dispatches sequentially to N providers; default trio preference Gemini + Grok (+ Mistral for security)
cloud_dispatch.check: rondo providers — show all configured providers with health status + latency

provider_health.description: Per-provider health checks with 5-min TTL cache (REQ-109 reqs 017-019)
provider_health.on_provider_down: logs WARNING, routes to fallback provider, never falls back to Claude interactive
provider_health.mcp: rondo_health (includes provider status when providers configured)

experiment_matrix.description: Model x effort x context x replicates comparison grids as ONE budgeted, resumable, blind-scorable run (REQ-113). Rondo's signature capability - no other dispatch tool has it.
experiment_matrix.cli: rondo matrix run exp.yaml [--dry-run] | status NAME | report NAME | reveal NAME
experiment_matrix.yaml_fields: name, prompt|prompt_file, inputs:{name:path} substituted at {{name}} placeholders (unresolved placeholders ABORT), models[], efforts[] (collapses to n/a for non-thinking models), contexts{name: file|none}, replicates, blind, baseline, budget_usd
experiment_matrix.budget: HARD ceiling: estimate-abort before any dispatch, running-stop mid-run (remaining cells = budget_exhausted, partials kept)
experiment_matrix.blind: group codes sealed with SHA-256; report shows codes until reveal; reveal verifies the seal
experiment_matrix.honesty_rules: self-ratings reported but labeled UNCALIBRATED and never ranked on; replicates report mean +/- stdev with a noisy flag
experiment_matrix.resume: re-running the same YAML skips done cells; error cells retry
experiment_matrix.notes: RONDO-317: judge scoring — judge: provider:model + judge_rubric (required); one external scorer rates every cell, costed into the same budget; report gains a judge column.

model_registry_drift.description: Self-updating model awareness (REQ-111 600-603): caches every provider's live model list, reports config-vs-served drift. Catches retired models BEFORE dispatches 404 (first run found the entire grok-3 family retired (now grok-4.3)).
model_registry_drift.cli: rondo providers --refresh (fetch+cache) | --drift (report OK/STALE/NO_CACHE per tier + NEW unconfigured models)
model_registry_drift.rule: NEVER auto-edits config - detection automated, fix manual

learned_scoring.description: 7-day learned per-model performance scores from Rondo's own audit history (REQ-109 300-324): success*0.5 + cost*0.3 + latency*0.2, min 10 dispatches.
learned_scoring.routing: recommend_model COALESCE: manual override -> curated default -> LEARNED best -> sonnet. Manual ALWAYS wins; [scoring] enabled=false disables; adaptive picks are logged, never silent

reliability_scoreboard.description: Windowed health truth (STD-101 240-242): 7-day and 30-day success rates + trend arrow vs the 95% target in rondo metrics and the morning report. Retry-queue depth alerts. Lifetime averages never bury current health.

failure_forensics.description: Every failed dispatch persists error_message (including the provider's HTTP error BODY), stderr snippet, blocked_reason, and project into the audit OUTCOME (STD-108 011-014 + STD-113 021-027). No failure is ever '(no message)'. Retry queue self-classifies transient vs permanent, dead-letters what can never succeed, ages out stale entries (STD-108 015-018).

thinking_models.description: Opus 4.8-era adaptive-thinking models handled automatically (REQ-109 200-211): temperature/top_p/top_k stripped, effort -> output_config (low/medium/high/xhigh/max), 32K output headroom (thinking tokens COUNT AGAINST max_tokens), 600s read timeout (max-effort thinking exceeds 120s). gpt-5*/o*-series get max_completion_tokens instead of max_tokens.
thinking_models.effort_axis: matrix efforts:[] sweeps thinking depth; non-thinking models collapse to one cell (no duplicate spend)

nightly_watchdog.summary: Schedulable watchdog sweep: registry drift + retry-queue sweep + 7d reliability vs the 95% target. Exit 0=green 1=alerts; macOS notification on alert.
nightly_watchdog.notes: RONDO-314, finding #285: the fleet had instruments but no night watchman. Subsystem crashes become alerts, never exceptions.

task_affinity.summary: Per-task model affinity: tag tasks with task_type; scoring learns (task_type, model) winners; recommend_model prefers the task-proven model over the global blend.
commands: task_type: code-review   # in round YAML tasks
rondo providers --scores  # watch the affinity table build
task_affinity.notes: RONDO-315, finding #297. COALESCE: override -> curated -> task-learned -> global-learned -> sonnet. Needs 10+ typed dispatches per pair in 7d.

auto_tiers_canary.summary: Auto-tiers derive auto_low/mid/high per provider from live catalogs (suggest mode, config never written); rondo models --verify canaries every configured tier with one tiny live dispatch.
commands: rondo models --tiers   # free, from registry cache
rondo models --verify  # live canary, ~cents, exit 1 on any FAIL
auto_tiers_canary.notes: RONDO-316, REQ-111 604-610. Manual pins always win. Non-chat models (embeddings/audio/video) excluded by token. First real canary: 15/15 PASS at $0.0007.

doctor.summary: rondo doctor: install diagnosis (config, keys last-4, registry drift, dirs, claude binary, versions) with PASS/WARN/FAIL + fix hints. --bundle writes a redacted support file; a leak aborts the write.
doctor.notes: RONDO-320, REQ-103 reqs 030-036. Zero dispatches, zero cost. Exit 0 healthy / 1 any FAIL (CI-able). Crashing checks become FAIL rows, never tracebacks.

config_options.name: max_budget_usd
config_options.description: Cost cap per task in USD (only works with auth:'api', not auth:'max' — Max plan has no per-token billing)

tasks.description: List of Task objects to execute

pre_gates.description: Gates checked BEFORE any task runs (all must pass)

post_gates.description: Gates checked AFTER all tasks complete

round_schema.note: Import: from rondo.engine import Round, Task, Gate

description.description: Brief human summary of what this task does

instruction.description: What Claude should do (the 'Do' field of three-field contract)

context_files.description: Files Claude should read for context (the 'Read' field)

auto_fn.description: Python function returning (bool, str) — alternative to three-field contract for non-AI tasks

mode.description: Task mode — 'interactive' dispatches to Claude, 'auto' calls auto_fn

task_schema.note: Interactive tasks need instruction + done_when. Auto tasks need auto_fn.

name.description: Gate name for logging and reporting

check_fn.description: Function returning (bool, str) — (passed, detail). Takes NO arguments. Capture context via closure.

blocking.description: If True and gate fails, round is aborted. If False, failure is logged but round continues.

example_round_file:
"""Example Rondo round file — copy this to get started.

Usage:
    rondo run this_file.py --dry-run      # preview
    rondo run this_file.py                # execute
    rondo run this_file.py --model haiku  # use haiku
"""

from rondo.engine import Round, Task


def build_round() -> Round:
    """Define the tasks for this round."""
    return Round(
        name="my-round",
        tasks=[
            Task(
                name="review-code",
                description="Review Python code for issues",
                instruction="""Review the code in src/ for:
1. Security issues
2. Performance problems
3. Missing error handling

Report findings as a numbered list.""",
                context_files=["src/main.py", "src/utils.py"],
                done_when="All files reviewed. Findings listed.",
            ),
            Task(
                name="check-tests",
                description="Verify test coverage",
                instruction="Run pytest and report coverage gaps.",
                done_when="Test results shown. Coverage gaps identified.",
                model="haiku",  # -- cheaper model for simple task
            ),
        ],
    )

important.model_parameter: Do NOT specify model= unless you need a DIFFERENT model than your current session. Omitting model= uses your current session model with zero overhead. Specifying a different model spawns a new process (slower).
important.dry_run: Always use dry_run=True first to preview what will be dispatched.
important.local_models: For cheap/fast tasks (review, classify, scan), use local Ollama models (e.g. model='llama3.1:8b') — $0 cost, ~2 seconds.
important.cloud_providers: Prefer Gemini + Grok + Mistral (core trio). Syntax: 'gemini:gemini-flash-latest', 'grok:grok-4.3', 'mistral:large' or tier 'mistral:high'. OpenAI is optional, not a default. Keys: ~/.rondo/config.toml or env.
important.multi_provider: Default multi-review pairs: Gemini + Grok (mid); security adds Mistral; reasoning uses Gemini 2.5 Pro + Mistral Large. rondo_cloud() and rondo_multi_review() run providers sequentially. Override with [routing.multi_review] in config.
important.health_fallback: Providers with health checks auto-fallback when down. Configure fallback= in [providers.<name>] section of config.toml. Check status with: rondo providers

description: AI dispatch layer — route tasks to any AI provider (Claude, Gemini, Grok, Mistral, Anthropic API, OpenAI, Ollama) and get structured results back. Supports single-provider dispatch, multi-provider sequential review (rondo_cloud / rondo_multi_review), and automatic health-based fallback.
deployment: Per-user, local infrastructure. Each user runs their own instance via Claude Code MCP stdio. No shared/multi-tenant mode.
